$schema-note: Machine form of docs/specs/STATE-COHERENCE-REGISTRY.md (the approved census, census-date 2026-06-05). AUTHORED, NOT build-generated (committed-generated-manifest conflict-loop lesson, COHERENCE-JOURNAL-SPEC §3.6). One entry per durable state category the census enumerates. Consumed by scripts/lint-state-registry.js: every direct durable-write site targeting a state dir / .instar/ must match an entry's `paths` (or carry an inline `/* state-registry: <category> */` annotation). Fields: category (kebab-case id) | description | scope (machine-local | must-be-coherent | derived-cache | coherent-on-demand) | freshness (real-time | eventual | session-boundary | n/a) | conflictShape (single-writer | append-only | lww | n/a) | transport (git | peer-http | none | deferred) | paths (glob-ish hints) | grandfathered (census marked uncertainty, §4e). New state declares its class here at birth.

entries.description: Per-machine append-only cross-machine event streams (topic-placement / session-lifecycle / autonomous-run); replicated first-hop over the mesh (COHERENCE-JOURNAL-SPEC P1).

retention.note: CARVED OUT — bounded inside CoherenceJournal via KindRetention + the new seq-floor prune guard; never externally rotated

entries.description: Durable working-set pending-pull ledger - outstanding fetches whose producer was offline/unreachable/revoked/mid-run; re-fired on reappearance/run-stopped (WORKING-SET-HANDOFF-SPEC P2 §3.4). Machine-local: records describe THIS machine's outstanding fetches; single-writer serialized mutate() funnel.

Durable (topic,epoch) operation-key window for working-set pull dedupe - restart-proof at-most-once scheduling per move (WORKING-SET-HANDOFF-SPEC P2 §3.3). Machine-local, bounded window (200 keys), single-writer (the coordinator).

Peer-visibility guard dedupe state - which improper revocations were already surfaced (keyed on revokedAt, cross-boot) + per-machine disappearance episodes for flap bounding (WORKING-SET-HANDOFF-SPEC P2 §3.6). Machine-local notice hygiene, never authority.

CommitmentTracker single-writer CAS store of user-facing promises and follow-through state. P1.5 (COMMITMENTS-COHERENCE-SPEC) fulfills the peer-http transport: owner-routed mutation + first-hop delta replication; the census's earlier 'AO desired' note is resolved-as-won't-do (owner-routing achieves coherence without the append-only conversion).

Per-peer read-only replicas of other machines' commitment stores (COMMITMENTS-COHERENCE-SPEC P1.5 §3.2). Derived cache - rebuilt by a full re-pull; written ONLY by the commitments-sync receive path; first-hop sender-bound; incarnation-fenced.

Durable owner-routed mutation intents awaiting an offline owner (COMMITMENTS-COHERENCE-SPEC P1.5 §3.4). Stores INTENT only - a FRESH signed envelope is re-issued at fire time; single-writer serialized funnel; per-(origin,id) and per-owner enqueue bounds; TTL 7d with one honest expiry notice.

Owner-side durable opKey replay window for commitment-mutate (COMMITMENTS-COHERENCE-SPEC P1.5 §3.4) - the replay control beyond the 60s envelope nonce window; records verdicts, survives restarts, TTL >= pending-mutation TTL.
Attention items surfaced to the user, plus the per-source coalescing/suppression ledger.

Operator-bound ack/resolve INTENT for a pooled attention item whose owner machine is briefly offline (WS4.1 follow-up, dark behind multiMachine.seamlessness.ws41DurableAck). Carries the authenticated operator principal; the owner revalidates it at apply time on return/boot. Append-then-compact, idempotent on (itemId,targetMachineId).

Observe-only ledger of Slack org permission-gate decisions (allow/clarify/refuse/step-up) with the verified principal + basis. Append-only; feeds false-positive-rate measurement before any enforcement is enabled. See docs/specs/SLACK-ORG-INTEGRATION-SPEC.md.
Durable pending Slack self-registration requests awaiting admin approval (SlackUserRegistry). Cleared on approve/deny. See docs/specs/SLACK-ORG-INTEGRATION-SPEC.md §6.3.

Durable per-principal behavioral baseline (Pillar 3 relationship-aware anomaly second factor): privacy-respecting SHAPE aggregates only — action repertoire, tier/hour histograms, coarse message-length stats, urgency rate — NEVER message content. Fed observe-only from authorized Slack traffic; read by RelationshipAnomalyScorer. Ships DARK. See docs/specs/SLACK-ORG-INTEGRATION-SPEC.md §7.1-7.2, §7.6.
Topic<->machine placement history (does not exist as its own store yet; the journal's first stream materializes it). Distinct from current-owner pool state.
Autonomous run working files and their declared artifact paths (the EXO stranding). Coherent-on-demand: fetched via working-set pull when a topic moves.
Threadline conversation store plus context-thread-map, thread-resume-map, telegram-bridge-bindings.
Cross-platform A2A message store, threads, and inbox/outbox JSONL indexes.
Telegram-topic-to-project bindings.
Per-person relationship records (one file per identity).
Multi-user identity / permissions registry.

Declarative job definitions (cron / prompt / supervision). The coherent half of jobs.json plus markdown job specs; run state is machine-local (see job-run-state). Field split required before whole-file sync (census §5).
Soul / identity narrative documents and self-knowledge facts (soul.md, identity.json, AGENT.md/USER.md/MEMORY.md/CLAUDE.md identity scaffold, self-knowledge facts).
Multi-spec project registry + plan docs + digest cache (claim-ownership verb already models owner-machine claim).
Learned preferences and correction-loop ledger.
Encrypted secret vault. The one category that truly replicates today (cross-machine secret sync, X25519-sealed push, receive-only default).
Current topic ownership (session-pool CAS + lease). The live single-writer source of truth for placement; the journal records its mutations.
Conversation live-tail replicated holder->standby per the seamlessness spec.

Fleet-wide config: feature flags, messaging[] config, monitoring tunables, multiMachine policy. SHARES config.json with machine-local fields (port, paths) -- split-required before whole-file sync (census §5).
Machine identity + signing/encryption keypairs. Identity IS the machine; never synced by definition.
Per-session state files. Sessions live and die on one machine; pool transfers create NEW sessions (CONTINUATION), not file copies.
Job RUN state: executions, active-job, run-history ledger. Runs are machine events (definitions are the coherent half, job-definitions).
Per-account-seat per-machine quota state. Broadcast-view ships via capacity heartbeats (quota-aware placement); the file itself is machine-local.
ResourceLedger CPU/RSS samples. Physical per-machine telemetry.
Lease / coordination state and registry liveness. The lease IS the cross-machine protocol; its state is the protocol's own.
NonceStore replay-protection watermarks. Per-channel per-machine by design.
Pending-relay and message-ledger SQLite queues. Outbound queue drains on the machine that owns the channel seat.
Telegram/iMessage/WhatsApp/Slack adapter state: poll offsets, chat.db, consent.json. Channel seat is held by one machine (lifeline); failover = seat transfer, not state merge.
Telegram/document attachment caches. Fetchable from the platform on demand.
Listener-daemon IPC internals: inbox/outbox.jsonl.active, pid, sentinels, hmac key. Daemon-local.
Threadline transport internals: relay-tokens, mcp-tokens, rate-limits. Connection-scoped; identity-keys are local-secret like machine keys.

Per-peer agent trust profiles (AgentTrustManager) + audit trail. Now CREDENTIAL-GATING (Secure A2A Verified Pairing §3.8): the profile carries the pairing lifecycle state (pending-verification / mutual-verified / verification-failed / identity-verified) that the credential-share gate keys on. MACHINE-LOCAL: the SAS-bound pending state + the machine-local handshake's identity decision live here; the SAS words themselves are in a separate 0600 store (pairing-pending.json, never in the registry). The VERIFIED-IDENTITY RESULT alone replicates via the threadline-pairing stateSync transport (see threadline-pairing-result) — this file is never whole-file-synced.

The replicated verified-IDENTITY RESULT of a Secure A2A pairing (Secure A2A Verified Pairing §3.8 / FD11). Replicates ONLY { peerFp, peerIdentityPub, state:'mutual-verified', verifiedAt, verifiedOnMachine } across the agent's OWN machines via the HLC replicated-store foundation (kind 'threadline-pairing-record') — NEVER the SAS words, shared secret, or relay token (those stay machine-local, bound to the machine-local handshake's ephemeral secret). Machine B honors a replicated record ONLY by pinning peerIdentityPub; a revoke/verification-failed propagates as a tombstone. Ships DARK behind multiMachine.stateSync.threadlinePairing (enabled:false, dryRun:true). Rides the coherence-journal stream files (no dedicated path).
Process/restart coordination: boot-id, restart markers, auto-updater state, caffeinate pid, slept-marker. Per-process lifecycle.
Per-boot/ephemeral caches: paste/, stop-gate.db, feature-registry.db, capability manifest. Recomputed or per-machine detection.
External-op trust elevation / incident state. Debatable whether trust transfers across machines -- flagged for P3+ review rather than silently local.
Destructive-ops audit stream (411M live -- needs rotation, census §7). Append-only per-machine.

retention.note: forensic trail — archive, never drop-delete (replaces SafeGitExecutor.maybeRotateAuditLog drop-rotate)

entries.description: Job-runs audit stream. Append-only per-machine.
Reaper decision audit stream (keep/kill transitions stamped with pressure tier).
Reap-log: why a session vanished (one JSON line per shutoff/refused-shutoff).
Sentinel-events audit stream (silently-stopped family transitions).
Security audit stream (auth/security events).

retention.note: forensic trail — archive, never drop-delete (replaces SecurityLog maybeRotateJsonl keep-ratio trim)

entries.description: Decision-journal audit stream.
Activity audit streams (date-partitioned, ~37M).
Watchdog-interventions audit stream.
External-operation gate operation-log audit stream.
Hash-chained trust-audit / mandate-decision chain.
Telegram message history JSONL (5.2M).
Agent-to-agent sent/received audit streams.
Prompt-gate audit stream.
Recovery-events audit stream (compaction/recovery lifecycle).
Skill-telemetry audit stream.
Feedback audit stream (Rising Tide feedback loop).
Apprenticeship lifecycle gate decisions audit stream.
Token ledger SQLite, rebuilt from Claude Code JSONL transcripts.
semantic.db / memory.db / episodes -- rebuildable from evidence. (Live-disk: stale since Jun 1 -- investigate separately, census §1.6/§7.)

retention.note: mixed .db + memory/semantic.jsonl; jsonl arm is A-class streamed in retrofit

entries.description: topic-memory.db (16M, active). Master spec open Q2: promote to COHERENT? -- left derived-cache until P3 review (census honesty).
Project-map spatial-awareness scan (refreshable).
Projects-digest cache.
docs-code-sync.json derived index.
MemoryIndex derived index over memory artifacts.
framework-model-preferences.db derived cache.
discovery.db derived cache (agent discovery).
state/instructions-tracking/ (59M, 15,180 UUID-keyed JSONL files). Census did not map an owner; volume says it matters. Owner + rotation + class needed (census §4e).
TopicIntentStore. Code references it; on-disk location unverified (census §4e).
shared-state.jsonl (SharedStateLedger). Nominally the git-synced cross-machine ledger -- is anything consuming it on real machines? May be the vestigial ancestor of the P1 journal (census §4e).
correction-capture-backlog.db vs correction-ledger.db: two stores, one sentinel -- which is canonical? (census §4e).
ProjectRoundWorktrees / TaskFlow stores. Routes-managed; durable form unverified (census §4e).
Stale legacy singletons (anti-patterns.json, quick-facts.json, project-registry.json, May 20 vintage, no live writers found). Retire per census §7.

Persisted context-exhaustion notify ledger - topicId -> {sessionName, at} already announced as dead, so the once-per-death-episode dedup survives server restarts (update-train churn previously re-announced the same death once per boot; 2026-06-06 residual on topics 16566/19437). Machine-local, single-writer (SessionMonitor), pruned at 7 days on load.

Pool Dashboard Streaming single-use bearer tickets (POOL-DASHBOARD-STREAM-SPEC §2.3). Minted by the pool-stream-ticket mesh verb, consumed once on the /pool-stream WS upgrade. Persisted so a captured ticket cannot replay across a serving-machine restart (consumed-set survives). Machine-local; single-writer (the store's own funnel); TTL-bounded + GC'd.

Boot-time guard posture snapshot written by the GuardPostureTripwire at every server boot; read by GET /guards for diverged-pending-restart derivation (GUARD-POSTURE-ENDPOINT-SPEC §2.2). Machine-local observability, never authority.

Durable last-known guard posture per peer machine with RECEIVER-side receipt time (GUARD-POSTURE-ENDPOINT-SPEC §2.3(c)) - survives local restarts so a dark peer's posture renders with its honest age. Written only at the heartbeat-ingestion chokepoint (registry-keyed identity). Machine-local observability, never authority.

GuardPostureProbe episode state - per-anomaly first/last-seen for the persist-across-ticks rule, flap windows, and the open-episode marker that keeps alerts to ONE aggregated Attention item per episode (GUARD-POSTURE-ENDPOINT-SPEC §2.4). Machine-local notice hygiene, never authority.

Durable, per-machine OWNED operator-accept records for the G3 dark-but-load-bearing classification (g3-dark-but-load-bearing-guards §2.4) - keyed <machineId>:<guardKey> -> {reason, owner, acceptedAt}. Suppresses a load-bearing guard's loadBearingGap into a VISIBLE accepted-risk row. Reserved for a genuine PIN-authenticated operator decision; per-machine (an accept on one machine never silences a peer's gap). Machine-local decision record, never authority over a peer.

retention.note: Bounded by the curated load-bearing guard set (currently small): one record per <machineId>:<guardKey>. Each record is RESOLVED by graduating the guard or by an operator DELETE-revoke; never grows unbounded.

entries.description: Relaying machine's append-only record of every relayed session-close ORDER (REMOTE-SESSION-CLOSE-SPEC §2.3) - target machine, sessionUuid, observed outcome. The owner's reap-log is the authoritative record of the kill; this is the order-end manifest so a relayed kill sweep cannot be traceless on the relayer. Machine-local audit history, never authority.

Durable mid-work resume queue + single-writer lockfile + decision audit (reap-notify spec R2.3): entries for autonomously-reaped mid-work sessions awaiting ordered revival, plus the resurrection tombstone ledger. Machine-local BY DESIGN — entries reference local tmux sessions/worktrees; deliberately EXCLUDED from backup/restore and never replicated (a foreign-host lock disables the queue loudly).

U4.5 RopeHealthMonitor durable debounce/episode memory (u4-5-rope-health-alerts §2) - per peer: condition, all-down onset, episodeKey, lastAlertAt, detected-not-notified flag. Transition-only writes with a short debounce; intra-episode counters lost to a restart are ACCEPTED (a restart re-debounces, never fabricates an episode). Machine-local BY DESIGN (rope health is per-machine-pair and directional); never authority.

retention.note: One record per known peer machine (peers x 1) - bounded by the machine registry size; a peer's record is overwritten in place on every transition, never appended.
